The important criteria include 'SpaceX Total Space Segment revenue was $3,557M in FY2023', which is a double-barreled/misleading inclusion because the critical section correctly notes that Space segment revenue reflects only external launches, making the total Space segment figure irrelevant/confusing for the customer launch calculation.
Falcon 9 payload capacity (22,800 kg to LEO) is listed as important but is never used in the rubric to contextualize the revenue difference, making its relevance unclear.
suggestions: Resolve the contradiction in the Rocket Lab FY2025 revenue per launch figure (is it $8.5M or $9.48M?) and ensure only the correct figure is listed as critical.
Promote the analytical conclusions about reusability and vertical integration from optional to at least important, since the question explicitly asks what the comparison reveals about these factors.

Remove 'SpaceX Total Space Segment revenue' from the important criteria or add a clarifying atomic note that this figure is distinct from Launch Services revenue, to prevent confusion with the calculation base.

Add a specific important or optional criterion addressing the size/class difference between Falcon 9 and Electron to contextualize why the per-launch revenue differs so significantly beyond just reusability.
